```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
set.seed(sample.int(.Machine$integer.max, 1))

mu0  <- sample(c(4, 6, 8), 1)
n    <- sample(c(25, 36, 49, 64), 1)
xbar <- round(runif(1, mu0 - 1, mu0 + 2), 2)
s2   <- round(runif(1, 1, 9), 2)

t_val   <- round(sqrt(n) * (xbar - mu0) / sqrt(s2), 4)
lrt_val <- round(n * log(1 + t_val^2 / (n - 1)), 4)

correct <- lrt_val
w1      <- round(t_val^2, 4)
w2      <- round(n * log(1 + t_val^2 / n), 4)
w3      <- round(lrt_val / 2, 4)
w4      <- round(2 * n * log(1 + abs(t_val) / (n - 1)), 4)

all_vals <- c(correct, w1, w2, w3, w4)

# Garante cinco alternativas numericamente distintas após a parametrização.
integer_choices <- all(abs(all_vals - round(all_vals)) < sqrt(.Machine$double.eps))
choice_step <- if (integer_choices) 1 else max(1e-4, max(abs(all_vals), na.rm = TRUE) * 0.01)
while (anyDuplicated(all_vals)) {
  duplicate_position <- which(duplicated(all_vals))[1]
  all_vals[duplicate_position] <- max(all_vals, na.rm = TRUE) + choice_step
}
choices <- sample(all_vals)
sol_pos <- which(choices == correct)
sol_str <- paste(as.integer(seq_along(choices) == sol_pos), collapse = "")
```

Question
========
Para $X_i \overset{\text{iid}}{\sim} N(\mu, \sigma^2)$ com $\sigma^2$ **desconhecido**, testamos $H_0: \mu = `r mu0`$.

O TRV tem estatística:
$$-2\log\Lambda_n = n\log\!\left(1 + \frac{T^2}{n-1}\right) \xrightarrow{D} \chi^2_1,$$
onde $T = \sqrt{n}(\bar{X} - \mu_0)/S$ é a estatística $t$ de Student.

Com $\bar{X} = `r xbar`$, $S^2 = `r s2`$, $n = `r n`$ (portanto $T = `r t_val`$), qual é o valor observado de $-2\log\Lambda_n$?

Answerlist
----------
* `r choices[1]`
* `r choices[2]`
* `r choices[3]`
* `r choices[4]`
* `r choices[5]`

Solution
========
**Passo 1:** calcular $T$:
$$T = \frac{\sqrt{`r n`}\,(`r xbar` - `r mu0`)}{\sqrt{`r s2`}} = `r t_val`.$$

**Passo 2:** calcular $-2\log\Lambda_n$:
$$-2\log\Lambda_n = `r n` \times \log\!\left(1 + \frac{`r t_val`^2}{`r n` - 1}\right) = `r n` \times \log\!\left(1 + \frac{`r round(t_val^2,4)`}{`r n-1`}\right) = `r lrt_val`.$$

**Nota:** $T^2 = `r round(t_val^2,4)`$ seria a estatística do teste $t$ ao quadrado (para $\alpha$ dado, seria comparada com $F_{1,n-1}$), mas **não** é o valor do TRV. O TRV usa o logaritmo conforme a fórmula acima.

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap7S2Q6_TRV_normal_sigma_desconhecido
extype: schoice
exsolution: `r sol_str`
exshuffle: FALSE
